Day 19 of my reincarnation, and this time we're tackling this — one of the most common keywords in JavaScript, and also one of the easiest to get burned by. This post focuses on what this actually is, why it points where it points, and how strict mode changes the picture.

JS From Zero (Day 18): localStorage — Save Data Right in the Browser

Day eighteen of the reincarnation. This time we look at another way to store data in the browser — localStorage. We'll cover what it is, how it differs from Cookie and sessionStorage, and how to set, read, delete, clear, and update values, plus a quick real-world example.
/en/javascript/2019/10/02/js-from-zero-ep17-cookie/
JS From Zero EP.17: How Cookies Actually Work in the Browser

Day seventeen of starting JavaScript from scratch. This post is all about cookies — what they are, the difference between memory cookies and disk cookies, how they pair up with sessions for login, and how to read and write them in the browser with document.cookie.
/en/javascript/2019/10/01/js-from-zero-16-operator-precedence-associativity/
Why Is 3 < 2 < 1 True in JavaScript? Operator Precedence and Associativity

Day 16 of the reincarnation. This post covers operator precedence and associativity in JavaScript. We'll look at why operators have a priority order, and the classic gotcha where 3 < 2 < 1 somehow evaluates to true. Once you understand associativity and type coercion, that weird result starts to make total sense.

RE: Learning JS From Zero — Day 15: Vanilla AJAX (XMLHttpRequest)

Day 15 of my reincarnated JS life. This time we dig into AJAX — what it actually is, why it changed the web, and how the native API XMLHttpRequest works under the hood. We'll also peek at the modern Fetch API and the old jQuery AJAX style so you can compare them side by side.
/en/javascript/2019/09/29/js-from-zero-14-json/
JS From Zero, Day 14: What Actually Is JSON (and Why It's Not JavaScript)

Day 14 of my JS-from-zero reincarnation, and this time we're tackling JSON. It looks a lot like JavaScript, but it's actually its own thing. We'll cover where it came from, why it beat out XML, and the two methods JavaScript gives you to convert between the two — JSON.stringify() and JSON.parse().
/en/javascript/2019/09/28/js-from-zero-ep-13-dom/
JS From Zero EP.13: What Is the DOM, Really?

Day thirteen of my JavaScript journey. This post is all about the DOM — the Document Object Model. We'll cover what the DOM tree is, what DOM elements, text, and attributes are, and why a NodeList isn't quite an array. It's the foundational concept every web dev needs to get.
/en/javascript/2019/09/27/js-from-zero-ep12-loops/
JS From Zero EP.12 — Loops: for, while, and do...while

Day twelve of being reborn into JavaScript, and this time we're talking about loops. We'll go through the three basic ones — for, while, and do...while — how they actually run, and the classic beginner trap of writing an infinite loop by forgetting to update the counter.
/en/javascript/2019/09/26/js-from-zero-ep-11-type-coercion/
RE: Learning JS From Zero — Day 11: Why Does 1 == '1' Return true?

Day 11 of the reincarnation. This time we dig into type coercion in JavaScript — one of the most common and most important concepts in the language. We'll talk about weak vs strong typing, why JavaScript loves to convert types behind your back, and why == can return some genuinely weird results.
/en/javascript/2019/09/25/js-from-zero-10-conditionals/
Learning JS From Zero — Day 10: if, else, and switch

Day 10 of the journey. This post is all about conditional statements — basically the if...else... family and switch. We'll cover how each one works, why an empty string counts as false, and the classic switch gotcha where forgetting break makes every case fall through.
